title: Error on T2W with expression for POI, on combine v10

posts.name: Beatriz Ribeiro Lopes
posts.text:
Dear all,
I recently upgraded to the new Combine version (v10.0.2), and now I can’t create workspaces using expressions for the POIs. For example, I have this command:
text2workspace.py chASYM_datacard.txt -o chASYM_datacard.root \
-P HiggsAnalysis.CombinedLimit.PhysicsModel:multiSignalModel --PO verbose \
--PO 'map=.*/signal0:r_ttg0[1,0.4,1.6]' \
--PO 'map=.*/signal1:r_ttg1=expr;;r_ttg1("1.010339*@0*(100+@1)/(100-@1)",r_ttg0,r_Ac[-0.5143048,-50,50])'

And the output is
Mapping  r_ttg0  to  ['.*/signal0']  patterns
Will create expression  r_ttg1  with factory  expr::r_ttg1("1.010339*@0*(100+@1)/(100-@1)",r_ttg0,r_Ac[-0.5143048,-50,50])
Mapping  r_ttg1  to  ['.*/signal1']  patterns
MH (not there before) will be assumed to be 0
free(): invalid pointer
*** Break *** abort

followed by a crash that is not helpful. Even if I simplify the expression a lot, e.g. just a sum, the result is the same, If I remove the expression and just scale a process directly, there is no error.
This same code was running well in combine v9. Does anyone have any idea of what could be the issue?
Thanks in advance,
Beatriz
posts.cooked_html:
Dear all,

I recently upgraded to the new Combine version (v10.0.2), and now I can’t create workspaces using expressions for the POIs. For example, I have this command:

text2workspace.py chASYM_datacard.txt -o chASYM_datacard.root \
    -P HiggsAnalysis.CombinedLimit.PhysicsModel:multiSignalModel --PO verbose \
    --PO 'map=.*/signal0:r_ttg0[1,0.4,1.6]' \
    --PO 'map=.*/signal1:r_ttg1=expr;;r_ttg1("1.010339*@0*(100+@1)/(100-@1)",r_ttg0,r_Ac[-0.5143048,-50,50])'

And the output is

Mapping  r_ttg0  to  ['.*/signal0']  patterns
Will create expression  r_ttg1  with factory  expr::r_ttg1("1.010339*@0*(100+@1)/(100-@1)",r_ttg0,r_Ac[-0.5143048,-50,50])
Mapping  r_ttg1  to  ['.*/signal1']  patterns
MH (not there before) will be assumed to be 0
free(): invalid pointer
 *** Break *** abort

followed by a crash that is not helpful. Even if I simplify the expression a lot, e.g. just a sum, the result is the same, If I remove the expression and just scale a process directly, there is no error.

This same code was running well in combine v9. Does anyone have any idea of what could be the issue?

Thanks in advance,
Beatriz

posts.name: Nicholas Wardle
posts.text:
Hi Beatriz,
Can you explain a bit more about the model you want to setup. I just tried a similar command in v10.0.2 on our toy hgg datacard and things work as expected (*). Can you send your datacard so that we can test with your setup?
Best,
Nick.
(*) I run the following
text2workspace.py data/tutorials/multiDim/toy-hgg-125.txt -m 125 -P HiggsAnalysis.CombinedLimit.PhysicsModel:multiSignalModel --PO verbose \
--PO 'map=.*/ggH:r_ggH[1,0.4,1.6]' \
--PO 'map=.*/qqH:r_qqH=expr::r_qqH("1.010339*@0+@1",r_ggH,r_Ac[-0.5143048,-50,50])'

and get,
Will create a POI  r_ggH  with factory  r_ggH[1,0.4,1.6]
Mapping  r_ggH  to  ['.*/ggH']  patterns
Will create expression  r_qqH  with factory  expr::r_qqH("1.010339*@0+@1",r_ggH,r_Ac[-0.5143048,-50,50])
Mapping  r_qqH  to  ['.*/qqH']  patterns
MH (not there before) will be assumed to be 125.0
Will scale  incl/ggH  by  r_ggH
Will scale  incl/qqH  by  r_qqH
Will scale  incl/bkg  by  1
Will scale  dijet/ggH  by  r_ggH
Will scale  dijet/qqH  by  r_qqH
Will scale  dijet/bkg  by  1

posts.name: Beatriz Ribeiro Lopes
posts.text:
Dear Nick,
Thanks for your quick reply. I just tried to run the same thing but on lxplus (was previously running on the DESY NAF) and there it works, I cannot reproduce the error that I get on the NAF. So I think it must be a problem with my setup there and not with Combine. I will just run on lxplus, sorry for wasting your time!
Cheers,
Beatriz
posts.cooked_html:
Dear Nick,

Thanks for your quick reply. I just tried to run the same thing but on lxplus (was previously running on the DESY NAF) and there it works, I cannot reproduce the error that I get on the NAF. So I think it must be a problem with my setup there and not with Combine. I will just run on lxplus, sorry for wasting your time!

Cheers,
Beatriz
